description: CLI interface for Adobe Podcast Enhance — upload audio, run AI speech enhancement, download result

enhance.usage_instructions: Use `adobe-podcast enhance` for audio enhancement commands. Available subcommands: `run`.

run.help: Upload a file, run Adobe Podcast Enhance, and download the enhanced result.
run.usage_instructions: Run `adobe-podcast enhance run <input_file>` to upload an audio or video file, run AI speech enhancement, and download the result. Output defaults to `<stem>-enhanced.wav` in the same directory. Use `--output/-o` to specify a different output path. Gain options control the mix: `--enhanced-gain` (AI-enhanced speech, default 1.0), `--background-gain` (background audio, default 0.0), `--isolated-gain` (isolated speech, default 1.0). All gains are 0.0–1.0. Examples: `adobe-podcast enhance run recording.wav`, `adobe-podcast enhance run recording.wav --output enhanced.wav`, `adobe-podcast enhance run podcast.mp3 --background-gain 0.2`.

auth.usage_instructions: Use `adobe-podcast auth` for authentication management. Available subcommands: `login`, `logout`, `status`, `test`, `profiles`.

login.help: Configure authentication credentials. Opens a browser window for manual Adobe IMS sign-in (CDP automation is blocked by Adobe).
login.usage_instructions: Run `adobe-podcast auth login` to authenticate. A real browser window opens for Adobe IMS sign-in — log in manually, then close or return. Subsequent `enhance run` calls extract the IMS token headlessly and cache it. Use `--force/-F` to clear the existing session and re-authenticate. Use `--profile/-p` to save to a named profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to save credentials to

options.name: --force
options.help: Clear existing ephemeral auth state and re-authenticate

logout.help: Clear stored credentials and browser sessions.
logout.usage_instructions: Run `adobe-podcast auth logout` to clear stored credentials and browser sessions. Use `--profile/-p` to target a specific profile.

options.name: --profile
options.help: Profile name to clear credentials from

status.usage_instructions: Run `adobe-podcast auth status` to check authentication status across all profiles. Use `--table/-t` for table format.

test.help: Test authentication by verifying credentials work across profiles.
test.usage_instructions: Run `adobe-podcast auth test` to verify that saved credentials are valid. Use `--table/-t` for table format, `--verbose/-v` for detailed checks.

profiles.usage_instructions: Use `adobe-podcast auth profiles` for profile management. Available subcommands: `list`, `get`, `create`, `select`, `delete`, `remove`, `rename`.

list.help: List all profiles and show their auth types and active state.
list.usage_instructions: Run `adobe-podcast auth profiles list` to list all authentication profiles. Use `--table/-t` for table format, `--limit/-l` to cap results, `--filter/-f` for field filtering (e.g. `active:eq:True`), `--properties/-p` for specific fields.

options.name: --limit
options.help: Maximum number of profiles to return

options.name: --properties
options.help: Comma-separated list of fields to include

get.help: Get details for a specific profile.
get.usage_instructions: Run `adobe-podcast auth profiles get <name>` to get details for a specific profile. Use `--table/-t` for table format.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to get details for

create.help: Create a new profile from .env.example template.
create.usage_instructions: Run `adobe-podcast auth profiles create <name>` to create a new named profile.

select.help: Activate a profile within its auth type.
select.usage_instructions: Run `adobe-podcast auth profiles select <name>` to activate a profile.

arguments.name: name
arguments.help: Profile name to activate within its auth type

delete.help: Delete a profile and its data.
delete.usage_instructions: Run `adobe-podcast auth profiles delete <name>` to delete a profile. Use `--force/-F` to skip confirmation.

remove.help: Delete a profile and its data.
remove.usage_instructions: Run `adobe-podcast auth profiles remove <name>` to delete a profile. Alias for `delete`. Use `--force/-F` to skip confirmation.

rename.help: Rename a profile, re-keying its secrets to the new profile name.
rename.usage_instructions: Run `adobe-podcast auth profiles rename <old> <new>` to rename a profile. Use `--keep-old` to preserve the original profile, `--force/-F` to skip confirmation.

arguments.name: new
arguments.help: New profile name (must not already exist)

options.name: --keep-old
options.help: Build and activate the new profile but leave the old profile dir and old secrets intact

cache.usage_instructions: Use `adobe-podcast cache` for cache management. Available subcommands: `clear`.

clear.usage_instructions: Run `adobe-podcast cache clear` to remove all cached responses.
